text: This review explores the fundamental importance, proposed mechanisms, and supporting evidence for five major multicomponent reactions (MCRs): the Biginelli, Hantzsch, Mannich, Passerini, and Ugi reactions. Despite their widespread use in green chemistry and bioactive molecule synthesis, the specific pathways through which these complex transformations occur often remain a subject of debate. We critically evaluate established mechanistic theories alongside contemporary findings from computational chemistry and advanced spectroscopic techniques, such as ESI-MS/MS and NMR. By addressing current controversies and identifying emerging trends, this manuscript aims to consolidate our mechanistic understanding of these reactions to aid in the rational design of more efficient catalysts and sustainable synthetic methodologies.
